design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate suburban street on bicycle, deliver newspapers to subscriber houses by throwing papers, avoid obstacles and hazards, complete route to advance to next day
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (limited papers per route) vs. scoring opportunities (bonus points for property damage); navigation precision vs. speed; subscriber retention vs. risk-taking
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Paper delivery boy performing daily rounds with skill and daring, discovering shortcuts and secrets in suburban neighborhood
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game rewards property destruction and chaos alongside core delivery objective; unique oblique projection perspective treats street as navigational puzzle; deliberately includes mundane childhood task as core gameplay rather than narrative frame

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives; crashing into obstacles costs one life; game ends when all lives exhausted, all subscriptions canceled, or Sunday training course completed
difficulty.checkpointing: Daily progression structure; seven levels corresponding to Monday through Sunday; completing training course advances to next day
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty modes at start (Easy Street, Middle Road, Hard Way) that multiply point values; Sunday papers thrown slower, increasing difficulty on final level; difficulty affects subscription management rather than obstacle patterns

progression.upgrade_system: Subscriber acquisition system: successful deliveries add subscribers (up to 10 maximum); failed deliveries or property damage cause subscription cancellation; Japanese version grants 2 subscribers for perfect delivery vs. 1 in international versions
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven sequential days unlock linearly; training course unlocks after completing street route each day
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty modes with different point multipliers
Subscriber count affects route composition and challenge
Optional training course with unlimited papers and ramp-based scoring
Regional differences (Japanese version has enhanced rewards for perfect play)

world_building_techniques: Subscriber/non-subscriber house distinction through visual marking (bright colors for subscribers)
Dynamic neighborhood changes based on delivery success (subscription gains/losses)
Training course as skill-proving narrative beat
Daily progression structure creates week narrative arc

faction_indicators: Status reflected in bright color coding on map
danger_signals: Multiple obstacle types: cars, skateboarders, rolling tires, neighborhood hazards
Unused arcade enemies (burglar, cat, motorcycle rider, angry lady, dog) suggest suburban danger themes
Street traffic as chaotic force opposing delivery mission
environmental_storytelling: Training course provides contextual skill progression (targets, ramps, obstacles)
Destructible elements (windows, tombstones) suggest social consequences of careless delivery
Sunday papers weighted heavier, indicating narrative progression toward challenge peak
